commonProblems.h2: Mitsubishi ASX Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Mitsubishi ASX's 1.8L diesel (4N13) and 2.0L petrol (4B11) dominate UK replacement demand, with EGR cooler and timing chain issues most frequently reported. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Mitsubishi ASX EGR Cooler Failure - Why 4N13 Diesel Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.rootCause: Exhaust gas recirculation cooler cracks internally, allowing coolant to mix with exhaust soot, causing blockages and overheating.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: EGR valve cleaning or replacement, cooler bypass
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; may not prevent recurrence on high-mileage units

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 4N13/4N14 with upgraded EGR components, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Long-term solution; addresses root cause with upgraded internals

problems.recommendation: On an ASX worth £4,000-£8,000, a rebuilt engine often represents better value than repeated EGR repairs. If caught early, an EGR blank may extend life, but once coolant contamination occurs, replacement is usually the economical choice.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 4N13 engine prices for your Mitsubishi ASX

problems.h4: Mitsubishi ASX Timing Chain Failure - Why 4B11 Petrol Engines Stretch
problems.rootCause: Timing chain tensioner loses pressure over time, allowing chain stretch that causes rattling, poor timing, and potential valve damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Timing chain, tensioner, guides replacement before catastrophic failure
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if caught early with no engine damage; 40k-60k mile extension

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 4B11/4B12 with upgraded chain components, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value once valve damage occurs; eliminates future chain concerns

problems.recommendation: If you hear rattling on cold start but the engine still runs smoothly, a preventative chain kit may be cost-effective. Once misfires or loss of power appear, valve damage is likely and a rebuilt engine offers better long-term value.
problems.cta: Get quotes for 4B11 Mitsubishi ASX engine replacement

problems.h4: Mitsubishi ASX Turbo Failure - Why 4N13/4N14 Diesels Lose Boost
problems.rootCause: Turbocharger oil seals wear, allowing oil into the intake or exhaust, causing smoke, loss of power, and potential engine damage from oil starvation.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Turbocharger refurbishment with new seals and bearings
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if engine internals undamaged; 30k-50k mile extension

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt diesel engine with upgraded turbo, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if oil contamination damaged bearings or pistons

problems.recommendation: If turbo failure is caught before oil starvation damages the engine, a turbo rebuild may suffice. However, once blue smoke or knocking appears, internal damage is likely and a rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo offers better peace of mind.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 4N14 diesel engine prices for your Mitsubishi ASX

problems.h4: Mitsubishi ASX Oil Consumption - Why 4B12 Petrol Engines Burn Oil
problems.rootCause: Piston ring wear and valve stem seal deterioration allow oil to enter combustion chambers, causing excessive consumption and potential catalytic converter damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Cylinder head removal, new piston rings and valve seals
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if block undamaged; may extend life 30k-50k miles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 4B12 with upgraded rings and seals, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best long-term solution; eliminates oil consumption and protects catalytic converter

problems.recommendation: On a 2.4L ASX, oil consumption exceeding 1L per 1,000 miles indicates significant wear. A rebuilt engine with upgraded components typically offers better value than repeated top-end repairs, especially if the catalytic converter is at risk.
problems.cta: Find rebuilt 4B12 Mitsubishi ASX engine deals

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mitsubishi ASX
finalCta.paragraph: From EGR cooler cracks to timing chain stretch, Mitsubishi ASX engines face well-documented failure points that can lead to costly repairs. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Mitsubishi ASX specialists, with every rebuilt engine including upgraded components to address the original failure point, bench testing, and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Nationwide delivery and supply & fit options mean you can get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mitsubishi ASX Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mitsubishi ASX and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

items.descriptor: Diesel engines dominated ASX sales in the UK, with the 1.8L 4N13 and 2.2L 4N14 offering strong economy but known EGR and turbo concerns on higher-mileage examples.

EGR cooler failure between 60k-90k miles on 4N13
High UK replacement demand on 1.8 DI-D and 2.2 DI-D variants
DPF issues on short-journey urban driving

engineYears.h2: Mitsubishi ASX Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Mitsubishi ASX production timeline (2010-2019), organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The ASX launched in the UK with three core engines: 1.6L petrol, 1.8L diesel and 2.0L petrol, establishing the GA platform's initial powertrain lineup.
keyChanges: Mitsubishi ASX first launched in UK market
Initial engine lineup: 4A92 1.6L petrol, 4N13 1.8L diesel, 4B11 2.0L petrol
knownFor: Strong initial sales driven by diesel variants
Early 4B11 timing chain tensioner concerns emerge
4N13 EGR cooler issues reported on higher-mileage examples
4B12 oil consumption noted on 2.4L units
years.cta: Get quotes for 2010-2012 Mitsubishi ASX replacement engines
years.ticker: ASX 1.8 diesel engine replacement • ASX 2.0 petrol timing chain • ASX 2.4 oil consumption fix

years.description: The 2.2L 4N14 diesel joined the lineup for higher-spec 4WD models, while minor updates addressed early reliability feedback on petrol units.
keyChanges: Continued focus on diesel sales in UK market
knownFor: 4N14 turbo failures begin appearing on higher-mileage examples
4B11 timing chain wear becomes more widely reported
Diesel variants continue to dominate UK used market
1.6L petrol seen as reliable but underpowered by some owners

faq.h2: Mitsubishi ASX Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Mitsubishi ASX engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A rebuilt Mitsubishi ASX engine typically costs £1,450-£3,500 supply-only, depending on engine code and variant. Diesel 4N13/4N14 units sit at the higher end, while petrol 4B11/4A92 are generally more affordable.
keyPoints: Prices vary by engine code, variant and supplier
Supply & fit adds £450-£950 labour on average
Reconditioned options can reduce upfront cost
12-month warranty standard on rebuilt units
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Mitsubishi ASX engine prices today

items.question: What engine codes fit the Mitsubishi ASX?
items.answer: The Mitsubishi ASX used petrol codes 4A92, 4B11, 4B12 and diesel codes 4N13, 4N14, 9HD across its 2010-2019 UK production run. Always verify your exact code via registration check.
keyPoints: 4N13: 1.8L diesel (most common UK variant)
4B11: 2.0L petrol (most reliable petrol option)
9HD: 1.6L diesel (PSA-sourced, 2015+)
Enter reg above to confirm your exact engine code
items.cta: Check your Mitsubishi ASX engine code instantly

items.question: Is the Mitsubishi ASX 1.8 diesel engine reliable?
items.answer: The 4N13 1.8L diesel is generally robust but suffers EGR cooler failure between 60k-90k miles. Rebuilt units with upgraded EGR components offer improved long-term reliability.
keyPoints: EGR cooler failure is the most common issue
Turbo seals can leak oil at higher mileage
Regular servicing extends engine life significantly
Rebuilt engines address known failure points
items.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt 4N13 Mitsubishi ASX engines

items.question: What causes Mitsubishi ASX timing chain failure?
items.answer: Timing chain stretch on 4B11/4B12 petrol engines occurs when the tensioner loses pressure, typically after 80k miles. Early rattling on cold start is the key warning sign.
keyPoints: Listen for rattling noise on cold startup
Oil changes critical for tensioner longevity
Preventative replacement cheaper than engine damage
Rebuilt engines include upgraded chain components
items.cta: Compare timing chain repair vs replacement costs

items.question: Can I replace my Mitsubishi ASX diesel with a petrol engine?
items.answer: Engine swaps between diesel and petrol are not recommended for the Mitsubishi ASX due to ECU, wiring, transmission and emissions compliance complexities. Stick with the original fuel type.
keyPoints: ECU and wiring harnesses are fuel-type specific
Transmission compatibility issues likely
MoT emissions testing would fail post-swap
Rebuilt same-fuel engine is the practical solution
items.cta: Find Mitsubishi ASX same-fuel replacement engines

items.question: How long does a Mitsubishi ASX engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only rebuilt engines typically dispatch within 1-3 working days. Full supply & fit services usually complete the replacement in 1-2 days once the engine arrives at the garage.
keyPoints: Engine build time: 1-3 working days average
Delivery: Next-day or 48-hour UK courier options
Garage fitting: 1-2 days depending on variant
4WD models may require additional time for transfer case
items.cta: Book Mitsubishi ASX supply & fit service today

items.question: What warranty comes with a Mitsubishi ASX replacement engine?
items.answer: All rebuilt Mitsubishi ASX engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Reconditioned and used engines may offer shorter terms - always confirm with your supplier.
keyPoints: 12-month unlimited mileage standard on rebuilt units
Warranty covers parts and labour for engine-related faults
Keep service records to maintain warranty validity
Supply & fit warranties often include installation
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi ASX engine warranty terms

items.question: Are Mitsubishi ASX engines compatible with other Mitsubishi models?
items.answer: Some Mitsubishi ASX engine codes like 4B11 and 4N13 were used across multiple Mitsubishi models, but compatibility depends on ECU, mounts and ancillaries. Always verify via registration or engine code.
keyPoints: 4B11 used in Outlander, Lancer and ASX
4N13 shared with Outlander and Lancer diesel
ECU coding and mounts may differ between models
Registration check ensures exact compatibility
items.cta: Verify Mitsubishi ASX engine compatibility now

items.question: How do I know if my Mitsubishi ASX needs a new engine?
items.answer: Major warning signs include excessive smoke, knocking noises, loss of power, or coolant/oil mixing. If diagnostics confirm internal damage, a rebuilt engine is often more economical than repair.
keyPoints: Blue smoke indicates oil burning or turbo failure
Knocking suggests bearing or piston damage
Coolant in oil points to head gasket or EGR failure
Get a specialist diagnostic before deciding
items.cta: Get a Mitsubishi ASX engine diagnostic quote

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in an older Mitsubishi ASX?
items.answer: If your ASX is otherwise in good condition and valued above £3,000, a rebuilt engine (£1,450-£3,500) often extends life cost-effectively versus buying a replacement vehicle.
keyPoints: Consider vehicle condition beyond the engine
Rebuilt engine cost vs ASX market value
Supply & fit adds convenience for non-DIY owners
Warranty protection adds peace of mind
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi ASX replacement vs purchase costs
